html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Fifteen — Part One

The Book of Melakhim Bet

MELAKHIM BET

The Second Book of Kings

Chapters 1 through 7

Eliyahu Taken Up — Elisha's Ministry — Na'aman Healed — The Aramean Siege

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

No complete DSS manuscript for Melakhim Bet — primary witness: Masoretic Text

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon — Melakhim Bet preserved in full

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 7 — ELIYAHU DEPARTS AND ELISHA BEGINS

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 1 Akhazyahu Falls — He Inquires of Ba'al-Zevuv — Eliyahu's Fire From Heaven

1 After the death of Akhav, Moav rebelled against Yisra'EL.

2 Now Akhazyahu fell through the lattice in his upper chamber in Samaria, and lay sick. So he sent messengers, telling them, Go, inquire of Ba'al-Zevuv, the god of Ekron, whether I shall recover from this sickness.

3 But the angel of YHWH said to Eliyahu the Tishbite, Arise, go up to meet the messengers of the king of Samaria, and say to them, Is it because there is no Creator in Yisra'EL that you are going to inquire of Ba'al-Zevuv, the god of Ekron?

4 Now therefore thus says YHWH, You shall not come down from the bed to which you have gone up, but you shall surely die. So Eliyahu went.

5 The messengers returned to the king, and he said to them, Why have you returned?

6 And they said to him, There came a man to meet us, and said to us, Go back to the king who sent you, and say to him, Thus says YHWH, Is it because there is no Creator in Yisra'EL that you are sending to inquire of Ba'al-Zevuv, the god of Ekron? Therefore you shall not come down from the bed to which you have gone up, but you shall surely die.

7 He said to them, What kind of man was he who came to meet you and told you these things?

8 They answered him, He wore a garment of hair, with a belt of leather about his waist. And he said, It is Eliyahu the Tishbite.

9 Then the king sent to him a captain of fifty men with his fifty. He went up to Eliyahu, who was sitting on the top of a hill, and said to him, O man of Creator, the king says, Come down.

10 But Eliyahu answered the captain of fifty, If I am a man of Creator, let fire come down from heaven and consume you and your fifty. Then fire came down from heaven and consumed him and his fifty.

11 Again the king sent to him another captain of fifty men with his fifty. And he answered and said to him, O man of Creator, this is the king's order, Come down quickly.

12 But Eliyahu answered them, If I am a man of Creator, let fire come down from heaven and consume you and your fifty. Then the fire of Creator came down from heaven and consumed him and his fifty.

13 Again the king sent the captain of a third fifty with his fifty. And the third captain of fifty went up and came and fell on his knees before Eliyahu and entreated him, O man of Creator, please let my life, and the life of these fifty servants of yours, be precious in your sight.

14 Behold, fire came down from heaven and consumed the two former captains of fifty men with their fifties, but now let my life be precious in your sight.

15 And the angel of YHWH said to Eliyahu, Go down with him — do not be afraid of him. So he arose and went down with him to the king

16 And said to him, Thus says YHWH, Because you have sent messengers to inquire of Ba'al-Zevuv, the god of Ekron — is it because there is no Creator in Yisra'EL to inquire of his word? — therefore you shall not come down from the bed to which you have gone up, but you shall surely die.

17 So he died according to the word of YHWH that Eliyahu had spoken. Yehoram became king in his place in the second year of Yehoram the son of Yehoshafat, king of Yehudah, because Akhazyahu had no son.

18 Now the rest of the acts of Akhazyahu that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. The primary textual witnesses are the Masoretic Text and the Orit Ge'ez. Chapter 1 opens Melakhim Bet with Akhazyahu's fatal inquiry of Ba'al-Zevuv — Lord of the Flies — rather than YHWH. The covenant question Eliyahu delivers — is it because there is no Creator in Yisra'EL? — is the theological indictment of the entire chapter.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Melakhim Bet in full. The third captain's prostration before Eliyahu — verse 13-14 — and his plea that his life be precious is read as the covenant recognition that humility before YHWH's prophet produces a different outcome than royal command. The fire that consumed the first two companies does not fall on the third.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness for Melakhim Bet. Eliyahu's identification by the messengers — verse 8 — he wore a garment of hair with a belt of leather — is the covenant description of the prophetic appearance that will identify Yokhanan the Immerser in the later tradition. The Tishbite's outward garment marks the covenant prophet.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — is it because there is no Creator in Yisra'EL that you are going to inquire of Ba'al-Zevuv? The question defines the covenant issue with precision. The king of Yisra'EL — whose people received the covenant at Sinai, whose kingdom was built on YHWH's promise to Dawid — sends to a Pelishti city's god for medical guidance. YHWH's answer is not healing but death. The covenant principle: to seek guidance from what is not YHWH when YHWH is available is the declaration that YHWH is insufficient. YHWH treats the declaration seriously and responds accordingly.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 2 Eliyahu Taken Up in a Whirlwind — Elisha Takes the Mantle — The Waters Healed — The Bears and the Boys

1 Now when YHWH was about to take Eliyahu up to heaven by a whirlwind, Eliyahu and Elisha were on their way from Gilgal.

2 And Eliyahu said to Elisha, Please stay here, for YHWH has sent me as far as Beit-El. But Elisha said, As YHWH lives, and as you yourself live, I will not leave you. So they went down to Beit-El.

3 And the sons of the prophets who were in Beit-El came out to Elisha and said to him, Do you know that today YHWH will take away your master from over you? And he said, Yes, I know it — keep quiet.

4 Eliyahu said to him, Elisha, please stay here, for YHWH has sent me to Yerikho. But he said, As YHWH lives, and as you yourself live, I will not leave you. So they came to Yerikho.

5 The sons of the prophets who were at Yerikho drew near to Elisha and said to him, Do you know that today YHWH will take away your master from over you? And he answered, Yes, I know it — keep quiet.

6 Then Eliyahu said to him, Please stay here, for YHWH has sent me to the Yarden. But he said, As YHWH lives, and as you yourself live, I will not leave you. So the two of them went on.

7 Fifty men of the sons of the prophets also went and stood at some distance from them, as they both were standing by the Yarden.

8 Then Eliyahu took his cloak and rolled it up and struck the water, and the water was parted to the one side and to the other, till the two of them could go over on dry ground.

9 When they had crossed, Eliyahu said to Elisha, Ask what I shall do for you, before I am taken from you. And Elisha said, Please let there be a double portion of your spirit on me.

10 And he said, You have asked a hard thing — yet, if you see me as I am being taken from you, it shall be so for you, but if you do not see me, it shall not be so.

11 And as they still went on and talked, behold, chariots of fire and horses of fire separated the two of them. And Eliyahu went up by a whirlwind into heaven.

12 And Elisha saw it and he cried, My father, my father. The chariots of Yisra'EL and its horsemen. And he saw him no more. Then he took hold of his own clothes and tore them in two pieces.

13 And he took up the cloak of Eliyahu that had fallen from him and went back and stood on the bank of the Yarden.

14 Then he took the cloak of Eliyahu that had fallen from him and struck the water, saying, Where is YHWH, the Creator of Eliyahu? And when he had struck the water, the water was parted to the one side and to the other, and Elisha went over.

15 Now when the sons of the prophets who were at Yerikho saw him opposite them, they said, The spirit of Eliyahu rests on Elisha. And they came to meet him and bowed to the ground before him.

16 And they said to him, Behold now, there are with your servants fifty strong men. Please let them go and seek your master. It may be that the Spirit of YHWH has cast him onto some mountain or into some valley. And he said, You shall not send.

17 But when they urged him till he was ashamed, he said, Send. They sent therefore fifty men. And for three days they sought him but did not find him.

18 And they came back to him while he was staying at Yerikho, and he said to them, Did I not say to you, Do not go?

19 Now the men of the city said to Elisha, Behold, the situation of this city is pleasant, as my lord sees, but the water is bad, and the land is unfruitful.

20 He said, Bring me a new bowl, and put salt in it. So they brought it to him.

21 Then he went to the spring of water and threw salt in it and said, Thus says YHWH, I have healed this water — from now on neither death nor miscarriage shall come from it.

22 So the water has been healed to this day, according to the word that Elisha spoke.

23 He went up from there to Beit-El, and while he was going up on the way, some small boys came out of the city and mocked him, saying, Go up, you baldhead — go up, you baldhead.

24 And he turned around, and when he saw them, he cursed them in the name of YHWH. And two she-bears came out of the woods and tore forty-two of the boys.

25 From there he went on to Mount Karmel, and from there he returned to Samaria.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. The translation of Eliyahu — taken up in a whirlwind by chariots of fire and horses of fire — is one of only two recorded translations without death in the covenant canon, the other being Khanokh in Bereshit 5:24. The Eliyahu tradition in the Qumran community was significant, particularly in the Mal'akhi 4 covenant expectation of his return.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 9 — Elisha asks for a double portion of Eliyahu's spirit — is read as the covenant request for covenant succession at the highest level. The double portion — the firstborn's inheritance in Devarim 21:17 — requests the prophetic inheritance of the son who will carry on the father's covenant work.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 11 — chariots of fire and horses of fire separated the two of them, and Eliyahu went up by a whirlwind into heaven — is one of the most vivid covenant translations in the Nevi'im. Elisha's cry — my father, my father, the chariots of Yisra'EL and its horsemen — is the covenant declaration that the true defense of Yisra'EL is the prophetic word, not military hardware.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 13-14 — Elisha takes up Eliyahu's cloak and strikes the water with it, asking, where is YHWH, the Creator of Eliyahu? The water parts. The covenant succession is confirmed not by Eliyahu's declaration or the prophetic community's recognition but by the repetition of the miraculous sign with Eliyahu's own instrument. The cloak is not magic — it is the covenant instrument of the prophet whose Spirit rested on Elisha. The question — where is YHWH, the Creator of Eliyahu? — is the covenant declaration of continuity: YHWH who worked through Eliyahu now works through Elisha.

ELISHA'S MIRACLES — CHAPTERS 3 THROUGH 7

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 3 Yehoram and Yehoshafat Against Moav — The Dry Valley Filled With Water — Moav's King Sacrifices His Son

1 In the eighteenth year of Yehoshafat king of Yehudah, Yehoram the son of Akhav became king over Yisra'EL in Samaria, and he reigned twelve years.

2 He did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, though not like his father and mother, for he put away the pillar of Ba'al that his father had made.

3 Nevertheless, he clung to the sin of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin — he did not depart from it.

4 Now Mesha king of Moav was a sheep breeder, and he had to deliver to the king of Yisra'EL a hundred thousand lambs and the wool of a hundred thousand rams.

5 But when Akhav died, the king of Moav rebelled against the king of Yisra'EL.

6 So King Yehoram marched out of Samaria at that time and mustered all Yisra'EL.

7 And he went and sent word to Yehoshafat king of Yehudah, The king of Moav has rebelled against me. Will you go with me to battle against Moav? And he said, I will go. I am as you are, my people as your people, my horses as your horses.

8 Then he said, By which way shall we march? Yehoshafat answered, By the way of the wilderness of Edom.

9 So the king of Yisra'EL went with the king of Yehudah and the king of Edom. And when they had made a circuitous march of seven days, there was no water for the army or for the animals that followed them.

10 And the king of Yisra'EL said, Alas, YHWH has called these three kings to give them into the hand of Moav.

11 But Yehoshafat said, Is there no prophet of YHWH here, through whom we may inquire of YHWH? Then one of the king of Yisra'EL's servants answered, Elisha the son of Shafat is here, who poured water on the hands of Eliyahu.

12 And Yehoshafat said, The word of YHWH is with him. So the king of Yisra'EL and Yehoshafat and the king of Edom went down to him.

13 And Elisha said to the king of Yisra'EL, What have I to do with you? Go to the prophets of your father and to the prophets of your mother. But the king of Yisra'EL said to him, No — it is YHWH who has called these three kings to give them into the hand of Moav.

14 And Elisha said, As YHWH of hosts lives, before whom I stand, were it not that I have regard for Yehoshafat the king of Yehudah, I would neither look at you nor see you.

15 But now bring me a musician. And when the musician played, the hand of YHWH came upon him.

16 And he said, Thus says YHWH, I will make this dry streambed full of pools.

17 For thus says YHWH, You shall not see wind or rain, but that streambed shall be filled with water, so that you shall drink, you, your livestock, and your animals.

18 This is a light thing in the sight of YHWH. He will also give the Moavites into your hand,

19 And you shall attack every fortified city and every choice city, and shall fell every good tree and stop up all springs of water and ruin every good piece of land with stones.

20 The next morning, about the time of offering the sacrifice, behold, water came from the direction of Edom, till the country was filled with water.

21 When all the Moavites heard that the kings had come up to fight against them, all who were able to put on armor, from the youngest to the oldest, were called out and were drawn up at the border.

22 And when they rose early in the morning, and the sun shone on the water, the Moavites saw the water opposite them as red as blood.

23 And they said, This is blood — the kings have surely fought together and struck one another down. Now then, Moav, to the spoil.

24 But when they came to the camp of Yisra'EL, the Yisra'ELites rose and struck the Moavites, till they fled before them. And they went forward, striking the Moavites as they went.

25 And they overthrew the cities, and on every good piece of land every man threw a stone until it was covered. They stopped every spring of water and felled every good tree, till only its stones were left in Kir-Khareseth, and the slingers surrounded and attacked it.

26 When the king of Moav saw that the battle was going against him, he took with him seven hundred swordsmen to break through, opposite the king of Edom, but they could not.

27 Then he took his oldest son who was to reign in his place and offered him for a burnt offering on the wall. And there came great wrath against Yisra'EL. And they withdrew from him and returned to their own land.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 3 introduces Elisha in his role as war prophet. The account of Elisha calling for a musician before prophesying — verse 15 — is the only instance in the Nevi'im of a prophet using music to bring on the prophetic state, connecting the covenant prophetic tradition to the musical worship of the Tehillim.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Elisha's conditional engagement — were it not that I have regard for Yehoshafat the king of Yehudah — verse 14 — is read as the covenant principle that the presence of a covenant-faithful king creates space for prophetic ministry even in the company of covenant-unfaithful rulers.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 27 — the king of Moav offered his oldest son as a burnt offering on the wall, and there came great wrath against Yisra'EL, and they withdrew — is one of the most theologically difficult verses in Melakhim. The text does not explain the wrath or who sent it. The coalition withdraws without completing the victory.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 16-17 — thus says YHWH, I will make this dry streambed full of pools — you shall not see wind or rain, but the streambed shall be filled with water. The covenant miracle mirrors the provision at the brook Kherit in Eliyahu's time — YHWH provides water in the wilderness without the natural preconditions. Verse 18 — this is a light thing in the sight of YHWH. The covenant formula of understatement: what appears impossible from the human vantage is declared easy from YHWH's perspective. The covenant provision of water in the desert is routine for YHWH — what is more is the military victory added to it.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 4 The Widow's Oil — The Shunammite's Son — Death in the Pot — One Hundred Men Fed

1 Now the wife of one of the sons of the prophets cried to Elisha, Your servant my husband is dead, and you know that your servant feared YHWH, but the creditor has come to take my two children to be his slaves.

2 And Elisha said to her, What shall I do for you? Tell me, what have you in the house? And she said, Your servant has nothing in the house except a jar of oil.

3 Then he said, Go outside, borrow vessels from all your neighbors, empty vessels and not too few.

4 Then go in and shut the door behind yourself and your sons and pour into all these vessels. And when one is full, set it aside.

5 So she went from him and shut the door behind herself and her sons. And as she poured they brought the vessels to her.

6 When the vessels were full, she said to her son, Bring me another vessel. And he said to her, There is not another. Then the oil stopped flowing.

7 She came and told the man of Creator, and he said, Go, sell the oil and pay your debts, and you and your sons can live on the rest.

8 One day Elisha went on to Shunem, where a wealthy woman lived, who urged him to eat some food. So whenever he passed that way, he would turn in there to eat food.

9 And she said to her husband, Behold now, I know that this is a holy man of Creator who is continually passing our way.

10 Let us make a small room on the roof with walls and put there for him a bed, a table, a chair, and a lamp, so that whenever he comes to us, he can go in there.

11 One day he came there, and he turned into the chamber and rested there.

12 And he said to Gekhazi his servant, Call this Shunammite. When he had called her, she stood before him.

13 And he said to him, Say now to her, See, you have taken all this trouble for us — what is to be done for you? Would you have a word spoken on your behalf to the king or to the commander of the army? She answered, I dwell among my own people.

14 And he said, What then is to be done for her? Gekhazi answered, Well, she has no son, and her husband is old.

15 He said, Call her. And when he had called her, she stood in the doorway.

16 And he said, At this season, about this time next year, you shall embrace a son. And she said, No, my lord, O man of Creator — do not lie to your servant.

17 But the woman conceived, and she bore a son about that time the following spring, as Elisha had said to her.

18 When the child had grown, he went out one day to his father among the reapers.

19 And he said to his father, Oh, my head, my head. The father said to his servant, Carry him to his mother.

20 And when he had lifted him and brought him to his mother, the child sat on her lap till noon, and then he died.

21 And she went up and laid him on the bed of the man of Creator and shut the door behind him and went out.

22 And she called to her husband and said, Send me one of the servants and one of the donkeys, that I may quickly go to the man of Creator and come back again.

23 And he said, Why will you go to him today? It is neither new moon nor Sabbath. She said, All is well.

24 Then she saddled the donkey, and she said to her servant, Urge the animal on — do not slacken the pace for me unless I tell you.

25 So she set out and came to the man of Creator at Mount Karmel. When the man of Creator saw her coming, he said to Gekhazi his servant, Look, there is the Shunammite.

26 Run at once to meet her and say to her, Is all well with you? Is all well with your husband? Is all well with the child? And she answered, All is well.

27 And when she came to the mountain to the man of Creator, she caught hold of his feet. And Gekhazi came to push her away. But the man of Creator said, Leave her alone, for she is in bitter distress, and YHWH has hidden it from me and has not told me.

28 Then she said, Did I ask my lord for a son? Did I not say, Do not deceive me?

29 He said to Gekhazi, Tie up your garment and take my staff in your hand and go. If you meet anyone, do not greet him, and if anyone greets you, do not reply. And lay my staff on the face of the child.

30 Then the mother of the child said, As YHWH lives and as you yourself live, I will not leave you. So he arose and followed her.

31 Gekhazi went on ahead and laid the staff on the face of the child, but there was no sound or sign of life. Therefore he returned to meet him and told him, The child has not awakened.

32 When Elisha came into the house, he saw the child lying dead on his bed.

33 So he went in and shut the door behind the two of them and prayed to YHWH.

34 Then he went up and lay on the child, putting his mouth on his mouth, his eyes on his eyes, and his hands on his hands. And as he stretched himself upon him, the flesh of the child became warm.

35 Then he got up again and walked once back and forth in the house, and went up and stretched himself upon him. The child sneezed seven times, and the child opened his eyes.

36 Then he summoned Gekhazi and said, Call this Shunammite. So he called her. And when she came to him, he said, Pick up your son.

37 She came and fell at his feet, bowing to the ground. Then she picked up her son and went out.

38 And Elisha came again to Gilgal when there was a famine in the land. And as the sons of the prophets were sitting before him, he said to his servant, Set on the large pot, and boil stew for the sons of the prophets.

39 One of them went out into the field to gather herbs, and found a wild vine and gathered from it his lap full of wild gourds, and came and cut them up into the pot of stew, not knowing what they were.

40 And they poured it out for the men to eat. But while they were eating of the stew, they cried out, O man of Creator, there is death in the pot. And they could not eat it.

41 He said, Then bring flour. And he threw it into the pot and said, Pour some out for the men, that they may eat. And there was nothing harmful in the pot.

42 A man came from Ba'al-Shalishah, bringing the man of Creator bread of the firstfruits, twenty loaves of barley and fresh ears of grain in his sack. And Elisha said, Give to the men that they may eat.

43 But his servant said, How can I set this before a hundred men? So he repeated, Give them to the men, that they may eat, for thus says YHWH, They shall eat and have some left.

44 So he set it before them. And they ate and had some left, according to the word of YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 4 is the richest Elisha miracle chapter in Melakhim Bet — the widow's oil, the Shunammite's son raised from the dead, the poisoned stew healed, and a hundred men fed from twenty loaves. The covenant pattern in each miracle: ordinary human insufficiency becomes the occasion for extraordinary covenant provision.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. The raising of the Shunammite's son — verses 32-37 — follows the same covenant pattern as Eliyahu's raising of the widow of Tzarefat's son in Melakhim Aleph 17: the prophet alone with the dead child, stretching himself over the body, praying to YHWH, the child reviving.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 26 — is all well with you? Is all well with your husband? Is all well with the child? And she answered, All is well — shalom, shalom, shalom. The woman whose son is dead in the prophet's chamber answers every inquiry with shalom. She is taking the crisis directly to the one who can address it, not dissipating it in secondary conversations.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 28 — did I ask my lord for a son? Did I not say, Do not deceive me? The Shunammite's grief becomes a covenant accusation: the miracle of birth was the prophet's word — the death of the child makes that word appear false. She holds Elisha to the covenant word spoken in verse 16. Her fierce covenant grief — verse 27, she caught hold of his feet — is the grip of a woman who will not release the prophet until the covenant word is fulfilled. Verse 44 — they ate and had some left, according to the word of YHWH. Twenty loaves for one hundred men, with remainder. The covenant covenant surplus that mirrors the manna in the wilderness.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 5 Na'aman the Leper — The Little Israelite Girl — Dip Seven Times in the Yarden — Gekhazi's Sin

1 Na'aman, commander of the army of the king of Aram, was a great man with his master and in high favor, because by him YHWH had given victory to Aram. He was a mighty man of valor, but he was a leper.

2 Now the Arameans on one of their raids had carried off a little girl from the land of Yisra'EL, and she worked in the service of Na'aman's wife.

3 She said to her mistress, Would that my lord were with the prophet who is in Samaria — he would cure him of his leprosy.

4 So Na'aman went in and told his lord, Thus and so spoke the girl from the land of Yisra'EL.

5 And the king of Aram said, Go now, and I will send a letter to the king of Yisra'EL. So he went, taking with him ten talents of silver, six thousand shekels of gold, and ten changes of clothing.

6 And he brought the letter to the king of Yisra'EL, which read, When this letter reaches you, know that I have sent to you Na'aman my servant, that you may cure him of his leprosy.

7 And when the king of Yisra'EL read the letter, he tore his clothes and said, Am I Creator, to kill and to make alive, that this man sends word to me to cure a man of his leprosy? Only consider, and see how he is seeking a quarrel with me.

8 But when Elisha the man of Creator heard that the king of Yisra'EL had torn his clothes, he sent to the king, saying, Why have you torn your clothes? Let him come now to me, that he may know there is a prophet in Yisra'EL.

9 So Na'aman came with his horses and chariots and stood at the door of Elisha's house.

10 And Elisha sent a messenger to him, saying, Go and wash in the Yarden seven times, and your flesh shall be restored, and you shall be clean.

11 But Na'aman was angry and went away, saying, Behold, I thought that he would surely come out to me and stand and call upon the name of YHWH his Creator, and wave his hand over the place and cure the leper.

12 Are not Avana and Pharpar, the rivers of Damascus, better than all the waters of Yisra'EL? Could I not wash in them and be clean? So he turned and went away in a rage.

13 But his servants came near and said to him, My father, it is a great word the prophet has spoken to you — will you not do it? Has he actually said to you, Wash, and be clean?

14 So he went down and dipped himself seven times in the Yarden, according to the word of the man of Creator, and his flesh was restored like the flesh of a little child, and he was clean.

15 Then he returned to the man of Creator, he and all his company, and he came and stood before him. And he said, Behold, I know that there is no Creator in all the earth but in Yisra'EL. So accept now a present from your servant.

16 But he said, As YHWH lives, before whom I stand, I will receive none. And he urged him to take it, but he refused.

17 Then Na'aman said, If not, please let there be given to your servant two mule loads of earth, for from now on your servant will not offer burnt offering or sacrifice to any god but YHWH.

18 In this matter may YHWH pardon your servant: when my master goes into the house of Rimmon to worship there, leaning on my arm, and I bow myself in the house of Rimmon, when I bow myself in the house of Rimmon, YHWH pardon your servant in this matter.

19 He said to him, Go in peace. So he departed from him a little way.

20 But Gekhazi, the servant of Elisha the man of Creator, said, See, my master has spared this Na'aman the Aramean, in not accepting from his hand what he brought. As YHWH lives, I will run after him and get something from him.

21 So Gekhazi followed Na'aman. And when Na'aman saw someone running after him, he got down from the chariot to meet him and said, Is all well?

22 And he said, All is well. My master has sent me to say, There have just come to me from the hill country of Efrayim two young men of the sons of the prophets. Please give them a talent of silver and two festal garments.

23 And Na'aman said, Be pleased to accept two talents. And he urged him and tied up two talents of silver in two bags, with two festal garments, and laid them on two of his servants. And they carried them before Gekhazi.

24 And when he came to the hill, he took them from their hand and put them in the house, and he sent the men away, and they departed.

25 He went in and stood before his master, and Elisha said to him, Where have you been, Gekhazi? And he said, Your servant went nowhere.

26 But he said to him, Did not my heart go when the man turned from his chariot to meet you? Was it a time to accept money and garments, olive orchards and vineyards, sheep and oxen, male servants and female servants?

27 Therefore the leprosy of Na'aman shall cling to you and to your descendants forever. So he went out from his presence a leper, like snow.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. The Na'aman narrative is one of the most theologically rich chapters in the entire Nevi'im — a foreign military commander's healing and covenant confession, mediated by an unnamed Israelite slave girl whose covenant witness sets the entire chain of events in motion.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 15 — behold, I know that there is no Creator in all the earth but in Yisra'EL — is read as the covenant declaration of a Gentile convert: Na'aman's healing produces a monotheistic covenant confession that exceeds the covenant faithfulness of most Israelite kings in Melakhim.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 17 — two mule loads of earth — Na'aman asks for soil from the covenant land to build an altar to YHWH in Aram. The theology embedded in the request reflects the ancient Near Eastern understanding of territorial gods — he wants to worship YHWH on YHWH's own ground, transported to Damascus.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 11-12 — Na'aman was angry because Elisha sent a messenger rather than coming out personally, and because the Yarden seemed inferior to the rivers of Damascus. The covenant healing required the humiliation of obedience without understanding. The seven dips in the Yarden accomplished nothing until the seventh — the covenant number of completion. His flesh was restored like the flesh of a little child. Verse 16 — Elisha refuses the payment. The covenant gift of healing cannot be monetized. Gekhazi's sin — verse 20-27 — is precisely the monetization of covenant grace that Elisha refused. The leprosy that clings to Gekhazi and his descendants forever is the covenant consequence of treating YHWH's healing as a commercial transaction.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 6 The Floating Axehead — The Blinded Arameans Led to Samaria — The Siege of Samaria — Famine

1 Now the sons of the prophets said to Elisha, See, the place where we dwell under your charge is too small for us.

2 Let us go to the Yarden and each of us get there a log, and let us make a place for us to dwell there. And he answered, Go.

3 Then one of them said, Be pleased to go with your servants. And he answered, I will go.

4 So he went with them. And when they came to the Yarden, they cut down trees.

5 But as one was felling a log, his axe head fell into the water, and he cried out, Alas, my master — it was borrowed.

6 Then the man of Creator said, Where did it fall? When he showed him the place, he cut off a stick and threw it in there and made the iron float.

7 And he said, Take it up. So he reached out his hand and took it.

8 Once when the king of Aram was warring against Yisra'EL, he took counsel with his servants, saying, At such and such a place shall be my camp.

9 But the man of Creator sent word to the king of Yisra'EL, Beware that you do not pass this place, for the Arameans are going down there.

10 And the king of Yisra'EL sent to the place about which the man of Creator told him. Thus he used to warn him, so that he saved himself there more than once or twice.

11 And the mind of the king of Aram was greatly troubled because of this thing, and he called his servants and said to them, Will you not show me who of us is for the king of Yisra'EL?

12 And one of his servants said, None, my lord, O king — but Elisha, the prophet who is in Yisra'EL, tells the king of Yisra'EL the words that you speak in your bedroom.

13 And he said, Go and see where he is, that I may send and seize him. It was told him, Behold, he is in Dotan.

14 So he sent there horses and chariots and a great army, and they came by night and surrounded the city.

15 When the servant of the man of Creator rose early in the morning and went out, behold, an army with horses and chariots was all around the city. And the servant said, Alas, my master — what shall we do?

16 He said, Do not be afraid, for those who are with us are more than those who are with them.

17 Then Elisha prayed and said, O YHWH, please open his eyes that he may see. So YHWH opened the eyes of the young man, and he saw, and behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha.

18 And when the Arameans came down against him, Elisha prayed to YHWH and said, Please strike this people with blindness. So he struck them with blindness in accordance with the prayer of Elisha.

19 And Elisha said to them, This is not the way, and this is not the city. Follow me, and I will bring you to the man whom you seek. And he led them to Samaria.

20 As soon as they entered Samaria, Elisha said, O YHWH, open the eyes of these men, that they may see. So YHWH opened their eyes, and they saw, and behold, they were in the midst of Samaria.

21 As soon as the king of Yisra'EL saw them, he said to Elisha, My father, shall I strike them down? Shall I strike them down?

22 He answered, You shall not strike them down. Would you strike down those whom you have taken captive with your sword and with your bow? Set bread and water before them, that they may eat and drink and go to their master.

23 So he prepared for them a great feast, and when they had eaten and drunk, he sent them away, and they went to their master. And the Arameans did not come again on raids into the land of Yisra'EL.

24 Afterward Ben-Hadad king of Aram mustered his entire army and went up and besieged Samaria.

25 And there was a great famine in Samaria, as they besieged it, until a donkey's head was sold for eighty shekels of silver, and the fourth part of a kab of dove's dung for five shekels of silver.

26 Now as the king of Yisra'EL was passing by on the wall, a woman cried out to him, Help, my lord, O king.

27 And he said, If YHWH will not help you, how shall I help you? From the threshing floor, or from the winepress?

28 And the king asked her, What is your trouble? She answered, This woman said to me, Give your son, that we may eat him today, and we will eat my son tomorrow.

29 So we boiled my son and ate him. And on the next day I said to her, Give your son, that we may eat him. But she has hidden her son.

30 When the king heard the words of the woman, he tore his clothes — now he was passing by on the wall — and the people looked, and behold, he had sackcloth beneath on his body.

31 And he said, May Creator do so to me and more also, if the head of Elisha the son of Shafat remains on his shoulders today.

32 Elisha was sitting in his house, and the elders were sitting with him. Now the king had dispatched a man from his presence, but before the messenger arrived Elisha said to the elders, Do you see how this murderer has sent to take off my head? Look, when the messenger comes, shut the door and hold the door fast against him. Is not the sound of his master's feet behind him?

33 And while he was still speaking with them, the messenger came down to him and said, This trouble is from YHWH — why should I wait for YHWH any longer?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 6 moves from the intimate covenant miracle of the floating axehead to the cosmic covenant vision of the armies of YHWH surrounding Dotan. Verse 17 — YHWH opened the eyes of the young man and he saw the mountain full of horses and chariots of fire — is the covenant declaration that the invisible covenant reality always outnumbers the visible human threat.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 16 — do not be afraid, for those who are with us are more than those who are with them — is read as the covenant declaration of the prophetic perspective: the servant sees the Aramean army encircling the city; Elisha sees YHWH's host encircling the Aramean army.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 22 — Elisha commands the king not to strike down the blinded Arameans but to feed them and send them home. The covenant treatment of the captive enemy produces the result of verse 23: the Arameans did not come again on raids. Covenant mercy achieves what military victory could not.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — YHWH opened the eyes of the young man, and he saw, and behold, the mountain was full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha. The same imagery as Eliyahu's translation in Chapter 2:11 — chariots of fire and horses of fire. The covenant protection of the prophet is the covenant protection of the word of YHWH he carries. Verse 25-29 — the famine of the siege drives Samaria to cannibalism. The covenant curse of Devarim 28:53-57 — you shall eat the fruit of your womb — is being enacted in Samaria. The woman's horror story does not produce covenant repentance from the king. It produces murderous rage against Elisha.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 7 The Four Lepers at the Gate — The Aramean Camp Abandoned — A Seah of Fine Flour for a Shekel

1 But Elisha said, Hear the word of YHWH: thus says YHWH, Tomorrow about this time a seah of fine flour shall be sold for a shekel, and two seahs of barley for a shekel, at the gate of Samaria.

2 Then the captain on whose hand the king leaned said to the man of Creator, If YHWH himself should make windows in heaven, could this thing be? But he said, You shall see it with your own eyes, but you shall not eat of it.

3 Now there were four men who were lepers at the entrance to the gate. And they said to one another, Why are we sitting here until we die?

4 If we say, Let us enter the city, the famine is in the city, and we shall die there. And if we sit here, we also die. So now come, let us go over to the camp of the Arameans. If they spare our lives we shall live, and if they kill us we shall but die.

5 So they arose at twilight to go to the camp of the Arameans. But when they came to the edge of the Aramean camp, behold, there was no one there.

6 For YHWH had made the army of the Arameans hear the sound of chariots and of horses, the sound of a great army, so that they said to one another, Behold, the king of Yisra'EL has hired against us the kings of the Khittites and the kings of Egypt to come against us.

7 So they fled away in the twilight and abandoned their tents, their horses, and their donkeys, leaving the camp as it was, and fled for their lives.

8 And when these lepers came to the edge of the camp, they went into a tent and ate and drank and carried off silver and gold and clothing and went and hid them. Then they came back and entered another tent and carried off things from it and went and hid them.

9 Then they said to one another, We are not doing right. This day is a day of good news. If we are silent and wait until the morning light, punishment will overtake us. Now therefore come — let us go and tell the king's household.

10 So they came and called to the gatekeepers of the city and told them, We went to the camp of the Arameans, and behold, there was no one to be seen or heard there — nothing but the horses tied and the donkeys tied, and the tents as they were.

11 Then the gatekeepers called out, and it was told within the king's household.

12 And the king rose in the night and said to his servants, I will tell you what the Arameans have done to us. They know that we are hungry. Therefore they have gone out of the camp to hide themselves in the open country, thinking, When they come out of the city, we shall take them alive and get into the city.

13 And one of his servants said, Let some men take five of the remaining horses, seeing that those who are left here will fare like the whole multitude of Yisra'EL who have already perished. Let us send and see.

14 So they took two horsemen, and the king sent them after the army of the Arameans, saying, Go and see.

15 So they went after them as far as the Yarden, and behold, all the way was littered with garments and equipment that the Arameans had thrown away in their haste. And the messengers returned and told the king.

16 Then the people went out and plundered the camp of the Arameans. So a seah of fine flour was sold for a shekel, and two seahs of barley for a shekel, according to the word of YHWH.

17 Now the king had appointed the captain on whose hand he leaned to have charge of the gate. And the people trampled him in the gate, so that he died, as the man of Creator had said when the king came down to him.

18 For when the man of Creator had said to the king, Two seahs of barley shall be sold for a shekel and a seah of fine flour for a shekel, about this time tomorrow in the gate of Samaria,

19 The captain had answered the man of Creator, If YHWH himself should make windows in heaven, could such a thing be? And he had said, You shall see it with your own eyes, but you shall not eat of it.

20 And so it happened to him, for the people trampled him in the gate and he died.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. The four lepers at the gate — verse 3-9 — are the covenant instruments through whom YHWH's provision reaches the starving city. The outcast, the unclean, the excluded are the covenant messengers of salvation. Their reasoning — verse 9 — this day is a day of good news. If we are silent and wait until morning, punishment will overtake us — is the covenant ethic of covenant testimony: received blessing must be shared.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. The fulfillment of Elisha's prophecy — verse 16 — a seah of fine flour for a shekel, according to the word of YHWH — and the death of the doubting captain — verse 17-20 — are read as the covenant double confirmation: YHWH's word of provision and YHWH's word of judgment both proved true on the same day.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 6 — YHWH had made the army of the Arameans hear the sound of a great army. The entire Aramean defeat is accomplished without a single Israelite sword. YHWH creates auditory hallucination in the Aramean camp that drives them to abandon everything and flee.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 9 — we are not doing right. This day is a day of good news. If we are silent and wait until morning, punishment will overtake us. Four lepers in a deserted enemy camp understand covenant responsibility before the king of Yisra'EL considers it. They have found abundance — silver, gold, clothing, food — and their first instinct is to hide and keep it. Their second thought is the covenant one: this is a day of good news and silence is not an option. The covenant obligation to share YHWH's provision falls even on the outcast.

END OF PART ONE — CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 7
